body.title: [sprint] uu readfeed <device> [channel] — generalize/retire readigor
body.intention: I intend that Akien can read any device's feed or logs with one zero-inference command, not just Igor's channel.
body.description:
**Problem:** readigor only reads Igor's channel. Generalize to `uu readfeed <device> [channel]` across all devices and channel types, retiring readigor.

**Shape:** `uu readfeed <device> [channel]`, default channel = personal. Channels: public (global rack route — all devices' public feeds), personal (web-chat feed), private (NOT designed — print explicit 'not yet designed' notice), console (INFO logs), debug (DEBUG logs). public/personal -> comms feed store (channel.py / web-chat); console/debug -> datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/. Maps onto the Murderbot feed metaphor (web UI) + the datacenter_logs hierarchy. Read-only.

**Affected files:** bin/uu (add readfeed verb); reads comms feed store + datacenter_logs/; remove readigor skill from skills/ + ~/.claude/skills/.
**Design rules:** zero-inference; read-only; feed metaphor; datacenter_logs hierarchy.
**Scope boundary:** IN — public/personal/console/debug readers + private stub + retire readigor. OUT — designing the private feed; any write/post path.
**Test plan:** `uu readfeed igor` shows Igor's personal feed; `uu readfeed igor debug` tails his debug log; `uu readfeed igor private` prints the not-designed notice; unknown device errors cleanly + nonzero.
**Completion criteria:** readfeed resolves all 4 live channels (public/personal/console/debug) for >=1 device; default (no channel) = personal; readigor removed from BOTH skill locations; no /readigor references remain.

## Akien 2026-06-24 — REPOINTED to the device model
feed is a DEVICE verb, not a top-level uu view: surface = `/device igor feed [channel]` and `uu device igor feed [channel]`. channels = personal (chat window / Murderbot feed), info (current console), debug (that device's debug log). Lives in devices/igor/bin/feed (zero-inference). FIRST verb on the device-dispatch mechanism — GATED on T-device-skills-via-uu-device. Retires readigor.
Completion criteria: `uu device igor feed` and `/device igor feed debug` return that device's channel; readigor deleted from both locations + manifest.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
